concerns.text: Tasks T037, T038, and T039 (Phase 6) address a 'Turing Review' concern regarding reaction-diffusion patterns and operational definitions. However, no such requirement, user story, or success criterion exists in spec.md or plan.md. These tasks are unanchored to any FR/SC/US, representing scope creep and a failure to map plan elements to spec requirements.

Task T012a defaults to loading synthetic data for the 'MVP' path. While plan.md acknowledges synthetic data for logic validation, spec.md FR-001 and US-1 explicitly require ingesting images from 'Allen Brain Atlas and AD Knowledge Portal'. The task's default behavior (synthetic) does not cover the mandatory spec requirement (real data ingestion) unless the 'override path' is triggered, but the task description prioritizes the synthetic path as the default implementation, leaving the FR-001 real-data ingestion logic potentially unimplemented or secondary.

Task T010a is marked conditional ('If unavailable... skip') to handle missing real manual annotations. Spec.md SC-001 requires extraction accuracy to be measured against manual ground truth to ensure <10% error. T010a admits this criterion cannot be met in the current phase and provides no task to schedule this verification once real data (Blocking Dependency 1) is acquired. The Success Criterion SC-001 is effectively unaddressed in the current task set.

Phase 6 tasks (T037-T039) reference 'Alan Turing' and 'reaction-diffusion' which are absent from the spec.md and plan.md. These tags/labels are stale or hallucinated references to an external review not present in the current project artifacts, creating confusion and untraceable work items.
